message:
I'll never forget the Sunday I realized our smoke alarms were... let's say "past their prime." Instead of stressing about it, I decided to make it a whole thing—put on a playlist, grabbed my partner and kids, and we turned it into this weird little safety treasure hunt around the house.

We tested every alarm, made a super basic escape plan (turns out our 8-year-old had some genuinely good ideas), and talked through what we'd actually do if there was ever a fire. No doom or gloom, just: "Here's what happens, here's where we go."

The funny part? Everyone actually *remembered* it because it didn't feel like a chore. It felt like we were all on the same team.

If you've been meaning to check your alarms or brush up on a plan, maybe try making it less "safety lecture" and more "quick cozy event." Honestly, it lands so much better.

What's one small safety thing you've been meaning to get around to? Has anyone else turned it into something less painful?

---

I found this refresher on kitchen fire safety and escape planning pretty helpful, too.
